purpose: This repository contains Apache Airflow DAGs (Directed Acyclic Graphs) for automating and orchestrating data workflows in a lending and selling system. It schedules and manages various ETL processes, data synchronization tasks, fee calculations, and integration points between different services in the lending platform.
businessFeatures: Loan data ingestion and curation from external sources

dataEntities.name: Loan
dataEntities.description: Core entity representing loan information including loan terms, status and related financial details

dataEntities.name: Account
dataEntities.description: Entity that tracks financial accounts associated with loans

dataEntities.name: Fee
dataEntities.description: Entity representing various fees applied to loans and accounts

dataEntities.name: MPL
dataEntities.description: Marketplace Lender entity representing organizations that originate loans

dataEntities.name: Organization
dataEntities.description: Entity representing issuing banks and investors

dataEntities.name: SOFR
dataEntities.description: Secured Overnight Financing Rate data used for interest rate calculations

dataEntities.name: LoanAction
dataEntities.description: Entity that tracks various actions performed on loans

dataEntities.name: Batch
dataEntities.description: Entity that manages groups of loans processed together

findings.title: Hardcoded credentials in configuration
findings.description: The Docker configuration files contain hardcoded database credentials (postgres:airflow) which could pose a security risk if exposed. These should be replaced with environment variables or secrets management.

findings.title: Tight coupling to AWS infrastructure
findings.description: The DAG implementations are tightly coupled to AWS services like ECS, SSM, and Secrets Manager, making it difficult to migrate to another cloud provider or run in a non-AWS environment. Consider abstracting cloud-specific implementations behind interfaces.
